commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No narrow code defect was found for automation to repair; the blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus normal maintainer review of the session-state risk.; Cleared: The diff only changes local error parsing, compaction parameter wiring, focused tests, and docs; it does not alter dependencies, CI, permissions, secrets, package resolution, or downloaded-code execution. (sha=55a6cb912b704573bcbc309bf4ea182c3c8aa24a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens provider overflow token-count parsing and forwards either the observed count or a synthetic over-budget count into overflow compaction, with focused tests and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. as a source-level reproduction. Current main classifies the reported provider-shaped overflow strings but does not extract their counts or synthesize a fallback count, so the focused PR tests cover a real current-main gap.
commands.review_followup: No narrow code defect was found for automation to repair; the blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus normal maintainer review of the session-state risk.

target.title: fix(agents): derive overflow budgets from provider errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Broaden observed overflow token extraction across provider-shaped error formats used during overflow recovery.
- Pass a minimally over-budget `currentTokenCount` into overflow compaction when overflow is confirmed but the provider message does not expose a parseable count.
- Add regression coverage and compaction docs for the shared overflow-budget path.
- Refresh the PR on current `origin/main` (`c8a35c4645dcf926d8e2c8165a1b0a2e48d24296`) and resolve the previous merge conflicts.

## Relevance check

Still relevant. Current `main` still classified Kimi/MiniMax/additive provider overflow messages as context overflow, but `extractObservedOverflowTokenCount()` only parsed older `prompt is too long: N tokens > ...`, `requested N tokens`, and `resulted in N tokens` forms. The runner still omitted `currentTokenCount` unless extraction succeeded, so confirmed-but-unparseable overflow could still fall through to insufficient budget compaction.

## Review comments addressed

- Removed the redundant `you requested ... tokens` regex from the old review path; it is not present in the refreshed branch.
- Refreshed the branch against current `main`, resolving the dirty/conflicting state.
- Updated the overflow diagnostic log to report both `observedTokens` and the effective `compactionTokens` so synthetic fallback counts are distinguishable from provider-reported counts.

## Verification

- Current head: `55a6cb912b704573bcbc309bf4ea182c3c8aa24a`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 201 passed (201)`.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview`
  - Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Provider-shaped context-overflow errors that already trigger overflow recovery now provide an effective overflow budget to compaction. Parseable provider counts are forwarded as `currentTokenCount`; confirmed overflow with no parseable count uses `ctxInfo.tokens + 1` so budget-targeted compaction still runs instead of acting as if there is no over-budget count.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on head `55a6cb912b704573bcbc309bf4ea182c3c8aa24a`, using the embedded runner overflow-compaction harness and the real overflow error classifier/parser tests. The branch was refreshed against `origin/main` at `c8a35c4645dcf926d8e2c8165a1b0a2e48d24296`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused node-wrapper Vitest command listed in Verification after formatting and before pushing. Also ran `git diff --check` and autoreview.

Evidence after fix: The parser test now asserts MiniMax-style `prompt is too long: 203557, model maximum context length: 196607`, Kimi-style `(requested: 291351)`, and additive `156321 + 48384 > 200000` overflow messages. The runner test now asserts confirmed unparseable overflow passes `currentTokenCount: 200001` into compaction. The focused test run passed with `Test Files 3 passed (3)` and `Tests 201 passed (201)`.

Observed result after fix: The refreshed branch is no longer conflicted (`mergeStateStatus` moved from `DIRTY` to `UNSTABLE` while new checks run). Overflow recovery now carries either the provider-observed count or the synthetic budget count into both the context-engine runtime context and the top-level compaction request.

What was not tested: A fresh live provider overflow against MiniMax/Kimi/opencode-go was not rerun, and the full repository build/check suite was not run locally. GitHub CI is running on the refreshed head.

## Notes

- Related to #70472, but does not fully resolve later session-rotation/orphaning behavior after failed compaction.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
